readings.title: The Chaser
readings.description: A witty, dark tale by John Collier about a young man seeking a love potion, exploring the complexities of desire, published in 1940.

readings.title: Sonnet 18
readings.description: A timeless sonnet by William Shakespeare celebrating love and poetry’s enduring power, written in 1609.

readings.title: Notes of a Native Son
readings.description: A powerful reflection by James Baldwin on race, family, and identity in America, published in 1955.

readings.title: A Worn Path
readings.description: A moving story by Eudora Welty of an elderly woman’s journey, rich in symbolism and resilience, published in 1941.

readings.title: The Road Not Taken
readings.description: A meditation by Robert Frost on life’s choices and their lasting impact, published in 1916.

readings.title: Notes on Camp
readings.description: An exploration by Susan Sontag of the aesthetics of 'camp' in art and culture, published in 1964.

readings.title: The Swimmer
readings.description: A surreal narrative by John Cheever of a man swimming through suburban pools, symbolizing loss and time, published in 1964.

readings.title: Ozymandias
readings.description: A sonnet by Percy Bysshe Shelley on the fleeting nature of power and human achievement, published in 1818.

readings.title: The Search for Marvin Gardens
readings.description: A blend of history and narrative by John McPhee about the origins of Monopoly, published in 1972.

readings.title: Tell-Tale Heart
readings.description: A chilling psychological tale by Edgar Allan Poe of guilt and madness, published in 1843.

readings.title: The Tyger
readings.description: A vivid exploration by William Blake of creation and divine mystery, published in 1794.

readings.title: Total Eclipse
readings.description: A philosophical account by Annie Dillard of witnessing a solar eclipse, published in 1982.

readings.title: Hills Like White Elephants
readings.description: A minimalist story by Ernest Hemingway about a couple’s tense conversation, published in 1927.

readings.title: Do Not Go Gentle into That Good Night
readings.description: A passionate villanelle by Dylan Thomas urging resistance against death, published in 1951.

readings.title: Against Joie de Vivre
readings.description: A humorous reflection by Phillip Lopate on the pursuit of happiness, published in 1986.

readings.title: The Lottery
readings.description: A shocking critique by Shirley Jackson of tradition and human nature, published in 1948.

readings.title: Because I Could Not Stop for Death
readings.description: A serene meditation by Emily Dickinson on mortality, published in 1890.

readings.title: The Fourth State of Matter
readings.description: A poignant narrative by Jo Ann Beard blending personal tragedy and science, published in 1996.

readings.title: The Mark of the Beast
readings.description: A tale by Rudyard Kipling of a British man cursed after defiling a temple in India, exploring superstition and colonial arrogance, published in 1890.

readings.title: Invictus
readings.description: An inspiring poem by William Ernest Henley about resilience and inner strength, written in 1888.

readings.title: Consider the Lobster
readings.description: A thought-provoking piece by David Foster Wallace on ethics and food culture, published in 2004.

readings.title: The Cage
readings.description: A sci-fi tale by A. Bertram Chandler about a human captured by aliens, exploring freedom and perspective, published in 1957.

readings.title: If—
readings.description: A concise, rhythmic poem by Rudyard Kipling on resilience and virtue, offering timeless wisdom, written in 1895.

readings.title: The Depressed Person
readings.description: A raw, introspective essay by David Foster Wallace on depression, blending humor and pathos in a compact, poignant narrative, published in 1998.

readings.title: A Good Man Is Hard to Find
readings.description: A dark, morally complex story by Flannery O’Connor about a family’s encounter with a criminal, published in 1955.

readings.title: Still I Rise
readings.description: An uplifting poem by Maya Angelou celebrating triumph over adversity, with lyrical power in a brief format, published in 1978.

readings.title: All Animals Are Equal
readings.description: A provocative excerpt from Peter Singer’s essay arguing for animal rights, with clear reasoning in a concise form, published in 1974.

readings.title: An Occurrence at Owl Creek Bridge
readings.description: A surreal Civil War story by Ambrose Bierce with a shocking twist, published in 1890.

readings.title: Fire and Ice
readings.description: A compact, potent poem by Robert Frost exploring destruction and desire, resonant in its brevity, published in 1920.

readings.title: On Seeing England for the First Time
readings.description: A sharp, personal essay by Jamaica Kincaid critiquing colonialism’s impact, delivered in vivid, concise prose, published in 1991.

readings.title: The Garden Party
readings.description: A lyrical story by Katherine Mansfield about class and mortality, published in 1922.

readings.title: This Is Just to Say
readings.description: A playful, deceptively simple poem by William Carlos Williams about plums and apology, rich in subtext, published in 1923.

readings.title: In the Belly of the Beast
readings.description: Barbara Kingsolver explores the U.S.'s relationship with military destruction, using a visit to a Titan missile silo as a backdrop, published in 2000.

readings.title: Strikebreaker
readings.description: A tense sci-fi story by Isaac Asimov about a worker breaking a caste strike on a confined space colony, probing social roles and sacrifice, published in 1957.

readings.title: Hope is the thing with feathers
readings.description: A vivid, brief poem by Emily Dickinson personifying hope as a bird, uplifting and concise, published in 1891.

readings.title: Slouching Towards Bethlehem
readings.description: A concise excerpt from Joan Didion’s iconic essay on 1960s counterculture, blending sharp observation and haunting tone, published in 1968.

readings.title: Nightfall
readings.description: A landmark sci-fi story by Isaac Asimov about a planet facing darkness, exploring societal collapse, published in 1941.

readings.title: A Dream Deferred
readings.description: A brief, powerful poem by Langston Hughes questioning the fate of postponed dreams, with vivid imagery, published in 1951.

readings.title: The White Album
readings.description: A long essay from Joan Didion’s reflecting on the 1960s, blending personal and cultural insights, published in 1968.

readings.title: The Open Window
readings.description: A witty, ironic story by Saki with a clever twist, published in 1914.

readings.title: Stopping by Woods on a Snowy Evening
readings.description: A serene poem by Robert Frost about pause and duty, concise yet deeply evocative, published in 1923.

readings.title: A Modest Proposal
readings.description: A biting satirical essay by Jonathan Swift proposing an outrageous solution to poverty, with sharp, concise prose, published in 1729.

readings.title: The Yellow Wallpaper
readings.description: A haunting psychological tale by Charlotte Perkins Gilman of a woman's mental unraveling under restrictive 'rest cure', published in 1892.

readings.title: Dulce et Decorum Est
readings.description: A powerful, unflinching anti-war poem by Wilfred Owen that exposes the horrors of trench warfare in World War I, published in 1917.

readings.title: Shooting an Elephant
readings.description: A compelling reflection by George Orwell on imperialism, authority, and moral pressure in colonial Burma, published in 1936.

readings.title: The Gift of the Magi
readings.description: A tender, ironic tale by O. Henry of a young couple's sacrificial Christmas gifts and true generosity, published in 1905.

readings.title: We Real Cool
readings.description: A rhythmic, jazz-inflected poem by Gwendolyn Brooks capturing the defiant lives of pool players, published in 1959.

readings.title: Once More to the Lake
readings.description: A nostalgic and poignant essay by E.B. White on memory, time's passage, and father-son bonds at a summer lake, published in 1941.

readings.title: There Will Come Soft Rains

readings.description: A haunting post-apocalyptic story by Ray Bradbury about an automated house persisting after nuclear war, published in 1950.

readings.title: The Lake Isle of Innisfree
readings.description: A lyrical poem by W.B. Yeats expressing a deep yearning for peace and solitude in nature, published in 1890.

readings.title: The Death of the Moth
readings.description: A delicate philosophical meditation by Virginia Woolf on life, death, and vitality witnessed in a moth's struggle, published in 1942.

readings.title: The Most Dangerous Game
readings.link: pdfs/The Most Dangerous Game by Richard Connell.pdf
readings.description: A suspenseful adventure tale by Richard Connell in which a hunter becomes the hunted on a remote island, published in 1924.

readings.title: Richard Cory
readings.description: An ironic poem by Edwin Arlington Robinson revealing the gap between appearance and inner despair, published in 1897.

readings.title: How It Feels to Be Colored Me
readings.description: A vibrant, insightful essay by Zora Neale Hurston on racial identity, individuality, and embracing life, published in 1928.

readings.title: The Necklace
readings.description: A masterful ironic story by Guy de Maupassant about pride, debt, and the high cost of appearances, published in 1884.

readings.title: My Last Duchess
readings.description: A chilling dramatic monologue by Robert Browning that exposes jealousy and power through a duke's words, published in 1842.

readings.title: A Hanging
readings.description: A gripping personal account by George Orwell of witnessing an execution and confronting its human reality, published in 1931.

readings.title: A Rose for Emily
readings.description: A Southern Gothic tale by William Faulkner exploring isolation, tradition, and decay in a small town, published in 1930.

readings.title: anyone lived in a pretty how town
readings.description: An inventive, rhythmic poem by e.e. cummings on the cycles of life, love, and indifference of society, published in 1940.

readings.title: Politics and the English Language
readings.description: A sharp critique by George Orwell of vague, pretentious language in politics and its dangers to thought, published in 1946.

readings.title: The Bet
readings.description: A profound philosophical story by Anton Chekhov examining the value of life through a high-stakes wager on isolation, published in 1889.

readings.title: Pied Beauty
readings.description: A joyful celebration by Gerard Manley Hopkins of God's dappled, varied creations in nature, published in 1877.

readings.title: The Crack-Up
readings.description: A candid, introspective essay by F. Scott Fitzgerald on personal crisis, resilience, and the price of success, published in 1936.
